label: keep
text: Avalonia DataGrid is a separate NuGet package; must dotnet add package Avalonia.Controls.DataGrid before using it.

label: keep
text: Salesforce: always check field-level security after an Apex deploy; profile/permset changes do not deploy automatically with classes.

label: keep
text: Never accept 'done' claims without independent verification: build success, targeted tests, and requirement-by-requirement pass/fail.

label: keep
text: Prefer streaming generators over building a full list when the input may be larger than memory.

label: keep
text: Windows PowerShell 5.1 reads .ps1 files as ANSI unless they have a UTF-8 BOM; use ASCII separators in script literals to avoid mojibake.

label: keep
text: Promote a memory to expert depth only after 3+ independent uses in different contexts; otherwise it is working at best.

label: keep
text: If a PR has 0 test changes and 100+ line diffs, request tests before reviewing the implementation.

label: keep
text: Prefer batched multi-replace over sequential single-replace edits when the edits are independent; one round-trip beats N.

label: keep
text: MuleSoft DataWeave: 'as Object {}' coerces to an empty object, not null; use 'default {}' on the upstream value if you need a null-safe empty.

label: keep
text: Before merging a PR that touches CI, run the new job locally against a known-good and known-bad input to confirm exit codes match expectations.

label: keep
text: git log -1 --format=%cs returns the committer date as a plain YYYY-MM-DD string; prefer it over %ci when you want a date-only field that does not drift across timezones.

label: keep
text: PowerShell closures capture variables by reference; mutating the variable after creating the closure changes what the closure observes when later invoked.

label: keep
text: Salesforce: a flow with a deactivated version still counts toward the per-org active-flow limit until it is deleted; deactivation alone does not free the slot.

label: keep
text: When CI green disagrees with local red, suspect timezone or line-ending normalization before suspecting flake; reproduce locally with TZ=UTC and unix line endings first.

label: keep
text: If a deploy needs more than one manual step in its rollback plan, the plan is wishful; either automate the steps or treat the deploy as irreversible.

label: keep
text: MkDocs strict mode treats a broken internal link as a build failure; run mkdocs build --strict locally before pushing so CI does not have to catch it.

label: keep
text: When a regex starts needing more than two lookarounds for a single rule, split the rule into a tokenizer plus a matcher; readability beats cleverness here.

label: keep
text: In Python, modifying a list while iterating it skips elements silently; iterate over list(xs) when you need to mutate the original during the loop.

label: keep
text: Salesforce: validation rules fire on flow-driven record updates the same as on UI updates; 'but it is a flow' is not an exemption.

label: keep
text: For irreversible operations, require two independent gates (build pass + targeted test pass on the impacted code) before sign-off; either gate alone is insufficient.

label: keep
text: In TypeScript, 'any' silently disables type checking for downstream chains; prefer 'unknown' so the compiler still forces a narrowing step before use.

label: keep
text: Salesforce Apex governor limit on SOQL is 100 queries per transaction; bulkify into one query with WHERE Id IN :collection instead of looping a SOQL inside a for loop.

label: keep
text: git rebase -i drops empty commits by default; pass --keep-empty if a merge-commit shape matters to your release tooling or downstream auditors.

label: keep
text: A CI cache keyed only on lockfile hash misses when transitive resolution changes upstream; include the registry config and the lock-tool version in the cache key.

label: keep
text: Reproduce a CI-only bug locally with act or a matching container before suspecting flake; flake is the diagnosis of last resort, not the first guess.

label: keep
text: A metric with no alert is decoration; either wire an alert with a real owner or delete the metric to stop pretending it is monitored.

label: keep
text: For an irreversible action behind a button, require a typed confirmation that matches the resource name; modal dialogs alone get muscle-memoried away within a week.

label: keep
text: When extracting a function, pass parameters explicitly even if the caller has them in scope; future readers cannot see what was implicit in the closure.

label: keep
text: A test that never fails is not a passing test, it is a missing test; add a known-bad input variant to confirm the assertion can actually fail.

label: keep
text: In Rust, the ? operator on a Result only converts via the From trait; if the error types do not have a From impl you get a confusing trait-not-satisfied message rather than a missing-conversion hint.
